frames: The agent begins in a low, crouched posture, then extends its right leg forward while lifting its left leg.
It transitions to a prone position, extending its body and right leg forward, with the left leg bent and lifted.
The agent pushes off with its right leg, bringing its left leg forward to make contact, maintaining a low crawl.
It continues to crawl forward, with its body close to the ground, alternating leg movements smoothly.
The agent maintains a low, elongated posture, extending its right leg while bending and lifting its left.
It propels itself forward with a smooth, undulating motion, keeping its torso flat on the surface.
The agent's right leg extends fully, while the left leg bends and prepares for the next forward push.
It concludes the movement in a low, stretched-out position, appearing to continue its crawling gait.
